code_template: Normalize counts per cell and apply log1p transformation.
Memory-First: Accepts both file path (str) and AnnData object.
Parameter Safety: Uses default_params merged with agent_params.
Metadata Footprinting: Records execution to adata.uns['analysis_history'].
Path to AnnData file or AnnData object in memory
AnnData with normalized and log-transformed expression values
adata = input_data if isinstance(input_data, ad.AnnData) else sc.read(input_data)

cognitive_layer.purpose: Normalization eliminates differences in sequencing depth between cells, making them comparable. After normalization, each cell has the same total counts. Log-transformation stabilizes variance, reduces the impact of outliers, and makes data more normally distributed for downstream analysis.

parameter_impact.target_sum: Target sum for normalization. 1e4 is common for 10x data (CPM-like). None uses median library size. Higher values scale up counts; lower values scale down.
parameter_impact.exclude_highly_expressed: When True, excludes highly expressed genes from normalization factor computation. Prevents outliers from dominating the scaling factor.

error_handling.ValueError: target_sum must be positive: Ensure target_sum is a positive number.
error_handling.ValueError: X has negative values: Data contains negative values. Log-transformation requires non-negative input. Check for prior transformations.

post_processing_code: return {'metrics': {}, 'warnings': ['scanpy_normalize not found in history'], 'success': False}
warnings.append('WARNING: Original data mean is <= 0. Check if data contains negative values or is already transformed.')
warnings.append('WARNING: Log-transformed data mean is <= 0. This may indicate all values were zero or negative.')
warnings.append('NOTE: High log mean detected. Consider checking if data is already log-transformed.')

counts_too_low.causal_chain: target_sum determines the library size each cell is normalized to. Higher values preserve more relative differences between genes.

counts_too_high.causal_chain: Lower target sum compresses expression values, which can help when downstream methods assume smaller ranges.
